target.title: fix(provider): route thinking profiles by model API
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes custom Anthropic-compatible providers whose selected model API is `anthropic-messages` but whose provider id is not literally `anthropic`.
- Carries the selected catalog/config model API into thinking policy resolution so Anthropic Messages routes can expose `adaptive`/`max`/`xhigh` levels according to the Claude model contract.
- Continues bundled API-family policy lookup across all deterministic owners when an earlier shared API owner declines, covering shared refs such as `anthropic-messages` and `openai-responses`.
- Keeps owner precedence intact: direct bundled provider policy still wins, exact runtime provider hooks can override API-family fallback, and API-family bundled policy only runs after provider-owned hooks decline.
- Out of scope: changing provider auth, model catalog contents, config shape, migrations, or transport behavior.

## Linked context

Closes #91975

Related: the issue's ClawSweeper review identified the missing API fact in thinking-policy dispatch and recommended reusing API-owner routing without provider-id substring matching. Follow-up ClawSweeper feedback also identified non-unique API refs, so this revision continues past declining owners instead of stopping at the first API-family match.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Custom providers with `api: "anthropic-messages"` now route thinking profile lookup through the Anthropic policy fallback after exact provider hooks decline, so supported Claude models no longer reject valid extended thinking controls such as `--thinking xhigh` solely because the provider id is custom.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw CLI/source checkout on Node 24, using the PR branch at commit `cde44b2362cb4f184e03c902050e093321f201ae` with dependencies installed.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` with provider `jdcloud-anthropic`, `api: "anthropic-messages"`, model `claude-opus-4-7`, then ran `pnpm openclaw agent --local --model jdcloud-anthropic/claude-opus-4-7 --message "thinking proof" --session-key agent:main:proof --thinking xhigh --json --timeout 3`. The proof command asserted that output contained the expected missing-auth error and did not contain `Thinking level "xhigh" is not supported`.
Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal capture from the local checkout:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/openclaw.json" OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$tmpdir/state" \
  pnpm openclaw agent --local --model jdcloud-anthropic/claude-opus-4-7 \
  --message "thinking proof" --session-key agent:main:proof --thinking xhigh --json --timeout 3

[diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main ... error="ProviderAuthError: No API key found for provider "jdcloud-anthropic". Auth store: [tmp-state]/agents/main/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite ..."
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=jdcloud-anthropic/claude-opus-4-7 candidate=jdcloud-anthropic/claude-opus-4-7 reason=auth next=none detail=No API key found for provider "jdcloud-anthropic" ...
FailoverError: No API key found for provider "jdcloud-anthropic" ...
PROOF_RESULT=passed: status 1 reached expected missing-auth path without unsupported-thinking error
```

Observed result after fix: The real CLI/local-agent entrypoint accepts `--thinking xhigh` for the configured custom `anthropic-messages` Claude Opus 4.7 route and proceeds to the expected auth failure. Before this fix, this path failed earlier with `Thinking level "xhigh" is not supported`.
What was not tested: No live external Anthropic/JDCloud API request was made; this change is local provider thinking-policy/profile resolution, not network transport or auth.
Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof intentionally uses a temporary config without credentials to avoid external network/API dependency; the expected terminal state is the missing-auth error after thinking validation succeeds.
Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Regression tests added in this PR encode the prior failure mode where the selected model API was available in catalog/config but was not passed through API-owner thinking-policy dispatch, and where the first shared API owner could block later owners.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/provider-thinking.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-public-artifacts.test.ts src/auto-reply/thinking.test.ts extensions/anthropic/provider-policy-api.test.ts extensions/openai/provider-policy-api.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check -- src/plugins/provider-public-artifacts.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts src/plugins/provider-thinking.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-public-artifacts.test.ts src/plugins/provider-thinking.test.ts extensions/openai/provider-policy-api.ts extensions/openai/provider-policy-api.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_LOCK=1 OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_PREPARE=1 node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugins/provider-thinking.ts src/plugins/provider-thinking.test.ts extensions/openai/provider-policy-api.ts extensions/openai/provider-policy-api.test.ts src/plugins/provider-public-artifacts.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-public-artifacts.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:docs` -> format, markdownlint, and MDX passed; `docs:check-i18n-glossary` reported pre-existing unrelated glossary gaps in other docs pages.
- `pnpm build`
- CLI proof command above, with grep assertions for expected auth failure and absence of unsupported-thinking error.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main` -> clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported.

Regression coverage added for API propagation, bundled policy lookup by manifest model-catalog API refs, shared API owner traversal for `anthropic-messages` and `openai-responses`, custom OpenAI Responses thinking profile opt-in, model-level API override precedence, runtime hook precedence over API fallback, and UI/autoreply thinking profile lookup through multiple API-ref owners.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No

Highest-risk area: Provider thinking-policy ownership and fallback ordering.

Risk mitigation: Direct bundled policy remains first for literal bundled providers, runtime provider hooks win before API-family fallback, active UI/autoreply provider hooks still win before bundled policy fallback, and common/non-unique API refs that decline no longer block later bundled owner policy surfaces.

## Current review state

Next action: Waiting for fresh CI and ClawSweeper re-review on head `e22ee99a5b96e6dcc19750a691a363aaca515de8`.

Still waiting on: Maintainer review and any broader CI/Testbox signal maintainers want beyond the focused local proof above.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: Rebased onto current `upstream/main`, dropped the already-merged `cliBackends` ownership overlap from #93261, fixed the two widened API literal test fixture type errors, refreshed current-head CLI proof, documented the new provider thinking-policy `api` context, and reran local autoreview clean.

target.title: fix(tls): add subjectAltName to auto-generated gateway self-signed certificate (fixes #96910)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96910.

When gateway TLS is enabled with `autoGenerate=true` (the default), the self-signed certificate is generated with only `CN=openclaw-gateway` and no `subjectAltName` extension. Node.js ≥17 ignores the CN for hostname/IP verification per RFC 6125, so the CLI probe to `wss://127.0.0.1:18789` fails with:

```
Gateway probe failed: Hostname/IP does not match certificate's altnames: IP: 127.0.0.1 is not in the cert's list:
```

## Why This Change Was Made

`openssl req -x509` was invoked without `-addext subjectAltName=...`, producing a CN-only certificate. Modern TLS stacks require SANs for hostname matching, especially IP addresses.

## User Impact

Users with `gateway.tls.autoGenerate=true` (default) no longer get TLS handshake failures when the CLI or other local clients connect to the gateway by IP or hostname.

Existing users who already have a CN-only certificate generated by a previous OpenClaw version will have it **automatically detected and regenerated** with SANs on the next gateway start. A warning log is emitted when this happens.

## Changes Made

- `src/infra/tls/gateway.ts`:
  - Added `import os` and `-addext "subjectAltName=..."` to `generateSelfSignedCert`
  - Added `isOpenClawGeneratedCnOnlyCert()` to detect old CN-only certs
  - Added `maybeUpgradeCnOnlyCert()` to regenerate old certs with SANs
  - Added `buildTlsResult()` helper to reduce duplication
- `src/infra/tls/gateway.test.ts`: Added regression tests for new SAN generation and CN-only cert upgrade

## Evidence

- **Unit tests:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/tls/gateway.test.ts` — 9/9 passed

## Real behavior proof

### Proof 1: New certificate contains SAN

```
$ openssl x509 -in gateway-cert.pem -noout -ext subjectAltName
X509v3 Subject Alternative Name:
    DNS:localhost, DNS:LIN-66D8BD4EA2C, IP Address:127.0.0.1, IP Address:0:0:0:0:0:0:0:1
```

```
$ node -e "... tls.checkServerIdentity(127.0.0.1, cert)"
✅ STRICT TLS CHECK PASSED for 127.0.0.1
✅ STRICT TLS CHECK PASSED for localhost
```

### Proof 2: Existing CN-only certificate is auto-upgraded

**Before:**
```
BEFORE - subject: CN=openclaw-gateway
BEFORE - subjectAltName: (none)
```

**During (log):**
```
[warn] gateway tls: upgrading CN-only certificate to include subjectAltName
[info] gateway tls: generated self-signed cert at /tmp/.../cert.pem
```

**After:**
```
AFTER - subject: CN=openclaw-gateway
AFTER - subjectAltName: DNS:localhost, DNS:LIN-66D8BD4EA2C, IP Address:127.0.0.1, IP Address:0:0:0:0:0:0:0:1
```

### Scope

- ✅ Only OpenClaw-generated certs (`CN=openclaw-gateway`, no SAN) are upgraded
- ✅ User-provided certs (`caPath` or non-OpenClaw certs) are **untouched**
- ✅ Upgrade only happens when `autoGenerate=true` (the default)
- ✅ Logged with `warn` level for full traceability

**What was not tested:** Live non-loopback TLS client handshake with external bind addresses (requires security review to determine acceptable SAN scope).

- [x] AI-assisted

target.title: fix(voice-call): emit canonical session keys
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: voice-call generated persisted session keys still used raw `voice:*` buckets, so multi-agent voice routing could diverge from the gateway's canonical `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` session key shape.
- Solution: generate canonical voice session keys with the normalized voice agent id while preserving explicit session key overrides.
- What changed: updated the voice-call session key resolver and refreshed manager/runtime/response-generator tests that assert generated persisted keys.
- What did NOT change: explicit session key overrides still pass through unchanged.
- Session-state upgrade boundary: existing raw generated `voice:*` and `voice:call:*` session-store entries are covered by the existing orphan-key migration path; the added proof below shows they migrate to the same canonical keys now generated by the resolver.

Fixes #83967

## Origin / follow-up

N/A — this is the existing PR #89884 for linked issue #83967, not a fan-out or follow-up to another merged PR.

## Maintainer readiness

- Fix classification: Root-cause source-of-truth session-state/data-format contract fix for generated Voice Call session keys.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High for the local resolver, manager persistence, embedded-agent session key, and raw generated session-store upgrade paths; live carrier/channel proof remains explicitly untested.
- Why it matters / User impact: Without canonical generated keys, Voice Call can persist generated state under raw `voice:*` buckets while the gateway/session store expects agent-scoped `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` keys, which can reset or split per-agent voice conversation state.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: `resolveVoiceCallSessionKey()` is the source-of-truth resolver for generated Voice Call session key identity, and the canonical session-state contract is `agent:<agentId>:voice:*`; this patch fixes that contract at the resolver boundary rather than masking it downstream.
- Related open PR scan / canonical PR check: Existing PR diagnostics for #89884 re-read the linked issue, PR discussion, review findings, diff, checks, and `check-upstream-fixed`; `origin/main` has not covered #83967 and the current maintainer signal is review of this PR's generated-key cutover/upgrade proof.
- Out of scope: This does not canonicalize explicit user-provided session keys, does not change live provider transport behavior, and does not add a new migration; it proves the existing orphan-key migration covers raw generated Voice Call session-store buckets.
- Patch quality notes: The diff is limited to one source file plus focused tests; no unrelated refactor, no repo temp files, no lockfile churn, and verification is bound to the current rebased HEAD by the daily-fix verification marker.
- Target test file: `extensions/voice-call/src/config.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/manager/outbound.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts`, and `extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.test.ts`.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The incorrect persisted identity originated at generated session-key resolution, so emitting canonical `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` keys from the resolver aligns every generated manager/runtime/response-generator caller with the gateway store namespace instead of masking the mismatch after persistence.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Generated voice-call manager paths now create and persist canonical `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` keys instead of bare `voice:*` keys, explicit session key overrides remain unchanged, and existing raw generated voice session-store entries upgrade to the same canonical keys.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout in `/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-89884` with Node/pnpm, the voice-call resolver and manager create/persist/reload paths, and the gateway session-store migration code loaded through `tsx`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/voice-call/src/config.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/outbound.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.test.ts
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/voice-call/src/config.ts extensions/voice-call/src/config.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/outbound.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:extensions
pnpm format:check -- extensions/voice-call/src/config.ts extensions/voice-call/src/config.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/outbound.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.test.ts
git diff --check
pnpm exec tsx inline proof script for generated resolver targets, manager create/persist/reload path, explicit override preservation, and raw generated session-store migration
```
- Evidence after fix: Console output from the local voice-call proof run:
```text
[voice-call] Inbound call accepted: policy is open
[voice-call] Created inbound call record: 7081fe33-fd29-48e3-a094-089ede756373 from +1 (555) 000-1111
[voice-call] Outbound call initiated: callId=2639a6fa-90e6-47c8-a9fc-6e6b1e9d7702 providerCallId=provider-call-2 mode=notify preConnectDtmf=no initialMessage=no
{
  "generatedResolverTargets": {
    "perPhone": "agent:voice:voice:15550001111",
    "perCall": "agent:voice:voice:call:call-123"
  },
  "managerCreatePersistReloadPath": {
    "activeInboundSessionKey": "agent:voice:voice:15550001111",
    "persistedInboundSessionKey": "agent:voice:voice:15550001111",
    "providerCallIdMappedToPersistedCall": true,
    "explicitOutboundSessionKey": "meet-room-1",
    "persistedExplicitOutboundSessionKey": "meet-room-1",
    "outboundSuccess": true
  },
  "rawGeneratedSessionUpgradePath": {
    "migrationChanges": [
      "Canonicalized 2 orphaned session key(s) in /tmp/openclaw-voice-call-proof-4Dyq8f/.openclaw/agents/voice/sessions/sessions.json"
    ],
    "migratedPhoneKey": "agent:voice:voice:15550001111",
    "migratedPhoneSessionId": "phone-session",
    "migratedCallKey": "agent:voice:voice:call:call-123",
    "migratedCallSessionId": "call-session",
    "rawKeysRemoved": true
  }
}
```
- Observed result after fix: The generated per-phone and per-call resolver targets are canonical agent-scoped keys; inbound manager state is active and persisted with the canonical key; outbound explicit `meet-room-1` is preserved; and the existing migration path canonicalizes two raw generated voice session keys while preserving their stored session ids and removing the raw `voice:*` entries.
- What was not tested: Live carrier/provider calls, Desktop, Mantis, Telegram, and other remote channel proof were not run in this local environment. The proof covers local voice-call resolver/manager/session-store behavior and discloses the live telephony boundary for maintainer revalidation.

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001: Upgrade proof is supplied above for existing raw generated Voice Call sessions. The proof seeds raw generated `voice:15550001111` and `voice:call:call-123` entries, runs the existing session-store migration path, and observes canonical `agent:voice:voice:15550001111` and `agent:voice:voice:call:call-123` keys with session ids preserved.
- RF-002: The session-state risk is still disclosed. The proof shows the raw generated agent session-store entries migrate to the same canonical keys now emitted by `resolveVoiceCallSessionKey()`, while explicit overrides remain unchanged.
- RF-003: No production code repair was needed after syncing with `origin/main`; the remaining decision is maintainer acceptance of the canonical generated-key cutover plus normal merge/CI gating.

## Merge risk

- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: session-state`, compatibility/data-format risk from changing generated persisted Voice Call session identity.
- Risk explanation: New generated Voice Call sessions move from raw `voice:*` / `voice:call:*` keys to canonical `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` / `agent:<agentId>:voice:call:*` keys. That is intentional because persisted generated voice state should live in the same agent-scoped namespace as the gateway session store.
- Why acceptable: Explicit session keys still pass through unchanged, so user-selected room/session identities are not rewritten. Existing raw generated session-store buckets are covered by the already-tested orphan-key migration path, and the local proof above verifies raw generated phone/call keys migrate to the new canonical targets with session ids preserved and raw keys removed.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: focused extension unit tests plus direct local manager/session-store execution.
- Target test files: `extensions/voice-call/src/config.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/manager/outbound.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: generated voice session keys include normalized agent id for default, configured-agent, per-phone, and per-call paths.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the resolver owns key generation, manager/runtime/response-generator tests verify generated keys reach persisted call and embedded-agent paths, and the migration proof covers pre-existing raw generated session-store buckets.

## Verification

```bash
node scripts/test-extension.mjs voice-call
```

After resolving the `origin/main` merge conflict on 2026-06-25, `node scripts/test-extension.mjs voice-call` passed in the PR worktree with 50 test files and 460 tests. The earlier full resolver/manager/session-store proof remains the behavioral proof for the generated session-key change; this run verifies the rebased conflict resolution against the current extension test suite.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The source resolver contract for generated Voice Call session state was wrong: `resolveVoiceCallSessionKey()` generated only the `voice:*` suffix and left agent scoping to later sandbox-session wrapping, while persisted voice call/session entries still used the raw key.
- Missing detection / guardrail: config tests covered per-phone/per-call suffixes but did not assert the canonical `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` persisted-key contract or the raw generated session-store upgrade boundary.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The resolver is the generation boundary for persisted Voice Call session identity, so canonicalizing there fixes every generated caller at the source while preserving explicit override behavior.

target.title: fix(signal): expose mention facts in inbound context
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Signal group mention gating now has native mention metadata available, but the delivered-turn decision facts are only visible inside the Signal monitor. Downstream prompt/tool context cannot inspect whether the inbound group message had native mention metadata, whether `requireMention` was enforced for that delivered turn, or which mention path matched the bot. That makes native-only group behavior hard to debug and forces consumers to infer channel-specific facts from text.

## Summary

- Exposes Signal mention-gating facts on the generic inbound message context.
- Adds `CanDetectMention`, `HasAnyMention`, `RequireMention`, `EffectiveWasMentioned`, and `MentionSource` for delivered native/text mention decisions.
- Keeps the Signal mention source literal typed so SDK/context consumers can rely on `"native"` vs `"mention_pattern"`.

## Stack

This is stacked after #96738 (`fix(signal): detect native bot mentions in group gating`). Until #96738 lands, this PR's diff will include that dependency.

## Production impact

No new outbound behavior. This only makes already-computed delivered-turn mention gating facts inspectable by prompts, tests, and plugin/channel contracts. Skipped Signal turns still return before prompt/tool context is built, so this PR intentionally does not expose a public skipped-turn context field.

## Evidence

- Focused Signal/channel tests cover native mention facts being projected into inbound context:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- Plugin SDK runtime mock tests cover `Mention*` parity with production inbound context.
- Broader affected-context regression suite passed:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts extensions/memory-core/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- Focused SDK mock test passed:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts`
- Plugin SDK API check passed:
  `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- Production TypeScript check passed:
  `pnpm tsgo:prod`

### Redacted Runtime Proof

Temporary local proof harness imported production `buildChannelInboundEventContext` and printed the emitted delivered prompt context on PR head `a681ab9967`. The proof preserves the computed mention facts while replacing all Signal identifiers, group identifiers, participant names, phone numbers, UUIDs, and message bodies with placeholders.

```console
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '<redacted proof harness>'
runtime-proof:environment
{
  "pr": 96862,
  "repo": "openclaw/openclaw",
  "head": "a681ab9967",
  "node": "v26.3.0"
}
runtime-proof:#96862 native mention facts emitted by delivered prompt context
[
  {
    "label": "native bot mention",
    "MentionSource": "native",
    "CanDetectMention": true,
    "HasAnyMention": true,
    "WasMentioned": true,
    "ExplicitlyMentionedBot": true,
    "RequireMention": true,
    "EffectiveWasMentioned": true,
    "MentionedUserIds": [
      "signal:<redacted-bot-uuid>"
    ]
  },
  {
    "label": "native mention of another participant",
    "MentionSource": "none",
    "CanDetectMention": true,
    "HasAnyMention": true,
    "WasMentioned": false,
    "ExplicitlyMentionedBot": false,
    "RequireMention": true,
    "EffectiveWasMentioned": false,
    "MentionedUserIds": [
      "signal:<redacted-other-participant-uuid>"
    ]
  }
]
```

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts extensions/memory-core/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`

target.title: Scope agent cron operations to the calling agent
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Agent-originated cron tool operations now carry the calling agent identity through the Gateway cron API, so cron reads and mutations made from an agent tool session are limited to that same agent's jobs.

## Why This Change Was Made

Cron APIs are also used by operator and internal flows, so this keeps the existing unscoped/operator behavior unchanged while adding an explicit scope only for agent-tool calls. The Gateway enforces the scope server-side, and the agent cron tool stamps newly created jobs with the calling agent before persistence.

## User Impact

Existing cron configurations and operator-managed cron jobs should continue to work normally. The new scope is only applied to agent-tool cron calls; direct unscoped cron RPC callers retain the current global behavior.

Agent-tool cron calls can still create, list, update, run, inspect history for, and remove jobs owned by the calling agent. Attempts from that path to target another agent's jobs or session references are rejected.

## Evidence

- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Focused local cron/protocol/tool tests:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/gateway-protocol/src/cron-validators.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/cron.validation.test.ts src/gateway/server.cron.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.flat-params.test.ts`

target.title: Check ClawHub trust before plugin and skill installs
target.body:
## Summary OpenClaw now checks ClawHub trust before installing or updating community plugins and skills, and blocks malicious or ClawHub-blocked releases before download. Suspicious community releases require explicit acknowledgement. Official ClawHub publishers and bundled OpenClaw sources bypass ClawHub risk prompts and built-in install friction. That covers official ClawHub plugins, ClawHub skills whose publisher metadata includes `owner.official: true` (the NVIDIA fixture proves this path), and bundled OpenClaw plugins/skills such as `openclaw/kitchen-sink` and `peekaboo`. Operator-owned `security.installPolicy` still runs for these sources so local admin allow/block policy is preserved. ## What Changed | Surface | Changed behavior | | --- | --- | | Community ClawHub plugins | Install/update reads ClawHub security and moderation state before download. Malicious or quarantined releases block; suspicious releases require acknowledgement. | | Community ClawHub skills | The same gate applies to archive-backed skill install/update, including owner-qualified refs. | | Official ClawHub plugins | `channel: "official"` / `isOfficial: true` sources install/update without ClawHub risk prompts, dependency-denylist scans, or before-install hooks. Operator `security.installPolicy` still runs. | | Official ClawHub skill publishers | `owner.official: true`, `owner.channel: "official"`, `owner.isOfficial: true`, `skill.channel: "official"`, or `skill.isOfficial: true` marks the default ClawHub skill source as official. The gateway `skills.detail` schema now preserves those fields for clients. | | Bundled OpenClaw plugin/skill sources | First-party bundled sources bypass trust prompts, dependency-denylist scans, and before-install hooks. Operator `security.installPolicy` still runs. | | Chat/control flows | Gateway protocol carries structured ClawHub trust errors so chat/control surfaces can distinguish blocked/suspicious installs from generic failures. | | Docs | Plugin, skill, update, and ClawHub CLI docs describe the community trust gate, acknowledgement flag, and official/bundled bypass. | ## Behavior Matrix | Source / release state | Install | Update | Operator policy / built-in friction | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Community malicious or quarantined plugin/skill | Blocks before download. | Blocks the latest release before replacing the installed version. | ClawHub block wins before local install surfaces. | | Community suspicious plugin/skill | Requires interactive acknowledgement or `--acknowledge-clawhub-risk`. | Same acknowledgement gate before update. | Operator policy and built-in install checks run after acknowledgement. | | Community clean plugin/skill | Installs normally. | Updates normally. | Operator policy and built-in install checks run normally. | | Official ClawHub plugin | Installs without trust prompt. | Updates without trust prompt. | Operator `security.installPolicy` still runs; dependency-denylist and before-install hook friction is bypassed. | | Official ClawHub skill publisher | Installs without trust prompt when official publisher metadata is present. | Updates without trust prompt when official publisher metadata is present. | Operator `security.installPolicy` still runs; dependency-denylist and before-install hook friction is bypassed. | | Bundled OpenClaw plugin/skill | Uses first-party source path. | Managed by bundled/product source path. | Operator `security.installPolicy` still runs; dependency-denylist and before-install hook friction is bypassed. | ## Proof Behavioral proof at PR head `491b6d1d525fdad51f5d2d5d1eb52c64fbf8825c`: - Community malicious plugin and skill fixtures return blocked ClawHub trust metadata; install exits non-zero before archive download, and update refuses to replace the installed version. - Community suspicious plugin and skill fixtures require acknowledgement; without acknowledgement the command exits non-zero, and with `--acknowledge-clawhub-risk` the same release installs or updates. - Official ClawHub plugin fixtures use `channel: "official"` / `isOfficial: true`; install succeeds with no ClawHub warning, while operator policy remains callable and built-in denylist/hook friction is skipped. - Official ClawHub skill fixture uses NVIDIA-shaped metadata: `owner: { handle: "nvidia", displayName: "NVIDIA", official: true }` for `tao-setup-nvidia-gpu-host`; the test asserts ClawHub security verdicts are not fetched and the install source is recorded as `{ kind: "clawhub", authority: "official", mutable: false, network: true }`. - The same official-skill logic is publisher-metadata based, not handle-hardcoded, so Peter Steinberger and OpenClaw skill publishers use the same path once ClawHub returns the official publisher field. - Bundled `openclaw/kitchen-sink` plugin and bundled `peekaboo` skill fixtures assert operator install policy is still called once, while dependency-denylist and before-install hook surfaces are not called. - `SkillsDetailResultSchema` now accepts the official skill/publisher metadata fields, so schema-validating gateway clients can receive the same trust signal that the install/update path consumes. - Owner-qualified skill trust fallback treats `/verify` Skill Card-only failures such as `card.missing` as non-security metadata when the security status is clean, so clean owner-qualified skills do not require risk acknowledgement only because a generated Skill Card is missing. - Owner-qualified skill trust fallback preserves missing exact-release versions unless the clean `card.missing` compatibility path is accepted, so `--acknowledge-clawhub-risk` cannot turn a missing exact-release verdict into an install. - Official and bundled bypasses preserve operator `security.installPolicy` while bypassing only ClawHub risk prompts and built-in dependency/hook friction. Production ClawHub rollout note: - Live production skill detail responses sampled earlier identified Peter/NVIDIA owners but did not yet expose `owner.official`. This OpenClaw PR consumes and preserves the field; ClawHub must expose it on skill detail/search responses for live official skill publishers to bypass friction in production. ## Screenshots These GitHub-hosted terminal screenshots are visual proof for the CLI prompt and block/acknowledgement states. The local proof generator was rerun at current PR head and regenerated the same 16-scenario transcript set; the images below remain the reviewer-visible screenshots for those states. | Screenshot group | Claim proved | Crop choice | | --- | --- | --- | | Plugin installs | Malicious blocks, suspicious acknowledgement gate, clean community install, and official/source-linked plugin install. | Element crop of terminal panel. | | Skill installs | Same trust gate for archive-backed skills, including owner-qualified labels and official/source-linked skill metadata. | Element crop of terminal panel. | | Plugin updates | Malicious latest release blocks download, suspicious latest release requires acknowledgement, and installed plugin remains unchanged when blocked. | Element crop of terminal panel. | | Skill updates | Same malicious/suspicious update behavior for archive-backed skills. | Element crop of terminal panel. |

target.title: feat(session): add named new and close chat commands
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Users can name sessions with `/name`, but starting a new named session from chat required an extra step. Chat users also had no direct command to close/archive the current non-main session, leaving session cleanup dependent on UI surfaces or backend tools.

## Why This Change Was Made

This PR extends session-management slash commands so `/new` can create a freshly reset, named session with explicit name syntax while preserving the existing `/new <model>` and post-reset prompt fallthrough behavior. It also adds `/close` and `/delete` for archiving the current non-main session through the gateway session lifecycle.

## User Impact

- `/new Beispielsessionname`, `/new --name Beispielsessionname`, `/new name:Beispielsessionname`, and quoted `/new "Planning notes"` can start a fresh named session.
- Existing model forms such as `/new gpt-5.5`, `/new opus`, and native provider/model values keep falling through to model selection.
- `/close` and `/delete` archive the active non-main session from chat, but refuse main sessions and require `operator.admin` for internal gateway clients.

## Related

- Addresses part of #93422 (`/new <name>`; `/name` already exists from #88581)
- Addresses the chat-side individual session close/delete part of #90757
- Complements #90655 and #88479 for session renaming surfaces
- Needs to be considered alongside #95182 if canonical `title` replaces/augments `label`

## Evidence

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/commands-delete-session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-delete-session.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-reset.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-reset-hooks.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/commands-delete-session.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-reset-hooks.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-name.test.ts` — 37 tests passed
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted with GitHub Copilot CLI. I reviewed the generated changes, resolved addressed Codex review conversations, and validated the touched command surfaces locally.

target.title: fix(systemd): reconcile managed-env unit migrations
target.body:
## Summary

- carry `#66295` onto current `main`
- fix the two real migration regressions from review: rebuild malformed units when `ExecStart=` is missing, and reconcile stale `WorkingDirectory=` in-place instead of leaving `CHDIR` failures behind
- remove the redundant managed-env split and clean up the temp-home test leakage

Fixes #66248.
Related #66219.
Supersedes #66295.

## Why this carry exists

`#66295` had the right core direction: move OpenClaw-managed systemd env into a drop-in so upgrades stop clobbering user `EnvironmentFile=` and `Environment=` directives.

The branch still needed maintainer follow-up in three spots:

1. malformed existing units with no `ExecStart=` were no longer repaired
2. stale `WorkingDirectory=` values could survive migration and fail startup at `CHDIR`
3. the new integration tests were leaving temp dirs behind

This carry fixes those and keeps the user-preserving drop-in design intact.

## Verification

- `pnpm test:serial src/daemon/systemd-unit.test.ts src/daemon/systemd.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`

## Changelog

Added under `## Unreleased` with:
- `Thanks @solomonneas.`
- `Carry follow-up: @vincentkoc.`

target.title: Fix session transcript path canonicalization on save
target.body:
# OpenClaw session-store path fix, 2026-04-12

## Status
Applied live to installed runtime. Also ported carefully into a fresh source clone of the matching `v2026.4.11` upstream tag, but not yet merged upstream or installed from that source tree.

## Source clone
- Repo: `/Users/rocklobster/.openclaw/workspace/external/openclaw-upstream`
- Branch: `fix/session-store-transcript-path-canonicalization`

## Protected file
`/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/store-Cw5RrVCw.js`

## Backup
`/Users/rocklobster/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/backups/runtime-patches/store-Cw5RrVCw.js.pre-20260412-1233`

## What changed
Added a save-time canonicalization step so persisted `sessionFile` and `transcriptPath` values are rewritten into the real sessions directory before session-store writes hit disk.

## Why
Without this, restored/recovered session entries could keep workspace-style transcript paths, then later be treated as missing/orphaned and get swept or misread.

## Verification performed
- syntax check passed
- synthetic save test passed
- gateway restarted cleanly
- post-restart audit:
  - `entries 913`
  - `legacy_non_agent_keys 0`
  - `non_cron_duplicate_sessionids 0`
  - `missing_jsonl_paths 0`

## Release blocker rule
Treat this as a release blocker until the fix is confirmed upstream and in the installed version. Before any OpenClaw update or manual overwrite touching runtime/config/session storage, stop and verify this protection first.

## Source patch details
Changed files in the source clone:
- `src/config/sessions/store.ts`
- `src/config/sessions/types.ts`
- `src/config/sessions/store.pruning.integration.test.ts`

Validation in source clone:
- `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts src/config/sessions/store.pruning.integration.test.ts`
- Result: `2 passed`, `29 passed`

## Patch export
- Mailbox-style patch export written for handoff/cherry-pick use:
  - `/Users/rocklobster/.openclaw/workspace/patches/openclaw-session-store-path-fix-2026-04-12.patch`

## Current git state
- Branch is clean and still anchored on tag base `v2026.4.11`
- Head commit:
  - `e1c0d1a` `Fix session transcript path canonicalization on save`

## Next upstream action
Open or stage an upstream PR from the source clone using commit `e1c0d1a` or the exported patch above. Keep the live runtime bandage in place until an official release is installed and re-verified on this machine.

target.title: fix: harden Mattermost slash callback auth
target.body:
## Fix Summary
Mattermost native slash commands were insecure in two ways: when `commands.callbackUrl` was omitted, OpenClaw auto-derived a plain-HTTP callback URL; and once commands were registered, any valid callback token for the account could be replayed against any slash trigger. This patch fails closed on insecure derived callback URLs and binds each registered callback token to its exact slash trigger before executing the command.

## Issue Linkage
Fixes #65624

## Security Snapshot
- CVSS v3.1: 7.6 (High)
- CVSS v4.0: 8.6 (High)

## Implementation Details
### Files Changed
- `docs/channels/mattermost.md` (+4/-1)
- `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` (+2/-0)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor-slash.test.ts` (+25/-11)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor-slash.ts` (+13/-0)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.send-config.test.ts` (+2/-0)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.test.ts` (+17/-2)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.ts` (+24/-1)
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-state.ts` (+12/-0)

### Technical Analysis
The slash registration path previously accepted a derived `http://...` callback when operators omitted `commands.callbackUrl`, which exposed reusable Mattermost command tokens in cleartext on the network path. Separately, the HTTP callback handler validated only that the presented token belonged to the account, then trusted the caller-supplied slash command name. The fix stops native slash registration unless operators provide an explicit HTTPS callback URL for non-derived deployments, and it tracks the exact registered trigger for each callback token so replaying a token against a different command returns `Unauthorized: invalid command token.`.

## Validation Evidence
- Command: `pnpm test extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.test.ts`
- Status: passed
- Command: `pnpm test extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.send-config.test.ts`
- Status: passed
- Command: `pnpm test extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor-slash.test.ts`
- Status: passed

## Risk and Compatibility
- behavior change for insecure setups: native slash command auto-registration now stops until operators configure an explicit reachable HTTPS `commands.callbackUrl`; valid callbacks keep working once configured

## AI-Assisted Disclosure
- AI-assisted: yes
- Model: github-copilot/gpt-5.4

target.title: Test/run state machine clamp guard
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: The `activeRuns` clamp guard in `onRunEnd()` (`Math.max(0, activeRuns - 1)`) was completely untested. Calling `onRunEnd()` more times than `onRunStart()` (e.g. error handling paths, race conditions) would cause `activeRuns` to go negative without this guard.
- Why it matters: A negative `activeRuns` would cause the machine to report incorrect state permanently, potentially breaking busy indicators across all channels.
- What changed: Added two unit tests covering the clamp guard — one for calling `onRunEnd()` with no prior `onRunStart()`, and one for calling it more times than `onRunStart()`.
- What did NOT change: No production code was changed. This is a test-only PR covering an existing defensive guard.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration

## Linked Issue/PR
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)
- Root cause: The `Math.max(0, activeRuns - 1)` guard in `onRunEnd()` was added defensively but never had test coverage to verify it works correctly.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No unit test for mismatched `onRunStart`/`onRunEnd` calls.
- Contributing context (if known): Error handling paths or race conditions can trigger `onRunEnd()` more times than `onRunStart()`.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `src/channels/run-state-machine.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `activeRuns` never goes below zero regardless of call order.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Pure function, no dependencies, directly tests the guard condition.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A — two tests added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
None.

## Diagram (if applicable)
```text
Before:
[onRunEnd x2] -> activeRuns = -1 (without guard, would break busy state)

After:
[onRunEnd x2] -> activeRuns = 0 (guard clamps correctly, now verified by test)
```

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification
### Environment
- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node.js
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A

### Steps
1. Call `machine.onRunEnd()` without calling `onRunStart()`
2. Check `activeRuns` value

### Expected
- `activeRuns` stays at 0

### Actual (before test)
- Guard existed but was unverified by any test

## Evidence
- [x] New unit tests added covering both scenarios

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: `onRunEnd` with no prior `onRunStart`, `onRunEnd` called twice after one `onRunStart`
- Edge cases checked: `activeRuns` stays at 0 in both cases
- What you did NOT verify: Race conditions in a live runtime

## Review Conversations
- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations
None.

target.title: Add Swedish control UI locale
target.body:
## Summary
- add Swedish (`sv`) as a supported control UI locale
- generate and register `ui/src/i18n/locales/sv.ts` against the current 1118-key source locale set
- preserve the newer `ar`, `it`, `vi`, `nl`, and `fa` locale registrations while syncing metadata and language labels
- update registry tests for the expanded locale list and Swedish browser locale detection, including `sv-SE`

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Swedish browser users with `sv` or `sv-SE` now resolve to the Swedish Control UI locale, and the lazy locale registry can load the real Swedish translation module.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `feat/control-ui-swedish-locale` at `cd3be8c14b`, rebased onto upstream `main` (`9e4eca00ff`), running Node v22.22.2 with repo dependencies installed by pnpm.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Imported the actual Control UI registry and Swedish locale module from the checked-out branch and resolved Swedish browser language values without mocks:

```console
node --import tsx --input-type=module -e 'import { resolveNavigatorLocale, loadLazyLocaleTranslation, SUPPORTED_LOCALES } from "./ui/src/i18n/lib/registry.ts"; const sv=await loadLazyLocaleTranslation("sv"); console.log(JSON.stringify({ hasSv: SUPPORTED_LOCALES.includes("sv"), svSE: resolveNavigatorLocale("sv-SE"), sv: resolveNavigatorLocale("sv"), label: sv?.languages?.sv, commonHealth: sv?.common?.health }, null, 2));'
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real local checkout:

```console
{
  "hasSv": true,
  "svSE": "sv",
  "sv": "sv",
  "label": "Svenska",
  "commonHealth": "Status"
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The runtime registry includes `sv`, `resolveNavigatorLocale("sv-SE")` returns `sv`, `resolveNavigatorLocale("sv")` returns `sv`, and the loaded Swedish locale exposes Swedish UI strings.

**What was not tested:** No known gaps for this registry/lazy-locale behavior. Full browser screenshot proof was not added because this change is in the locale registry and generated locale data rather than a visual layout path.

## Validation
- `pnpm ui:i18n:check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/i18n/registry.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- committed diff credential-pattern scan found no secret material

## Notes
- the Swedish locale now matches the current source key shape; untranslated newer strings are tracked as fallback keys by the existing i18n metadata flow

target.title: feat(google): add Google Vertex AI provider with ADC auth and global endpoint routing
target.body:
## Summary

- Registers a new `google-vertex` provider in the Google plugin that routes to `aiplatform.googleapis.com` using Application Default Credentials (ADC), separate from the existing `google-gemini-cli` OAuth path
- Adds `vertex-region.ts` for region/project/baseUrl resolution (env vars + ADC file fallback), with `global` location producing the unprefixed `aiplatform.googleapis.com` endpoint
- Adds `vertex-provider-catalog.ts` with the Gemini 3.x model catalog for Vertex AI
- Updates the Google transport stream to detect Vertex endpoints and construct the correct project/location URL path (`/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/publishers/google/models/{model}:streamGenerateContent`)
- Exchanges ADC refresh token for a Bearer token at request time, handling the pi-ai `"<authenticated>"` sentinel that was previously sent as a literal API key causing 401s
- Captures `thought_signature` from Gemini 3 thinking-mode tool call responses so multi-turn conversations replay correctly (fixes 400 `INVALID_ARGUMENT` on second turn)
- Adds `GCP_VERTEX_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_MARKER` sentinel and registers `google-vertex` in `openclaw.plugin.json`

Closes #49039
Closes #56253
Closes #58775
Closes #60736

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/google/vertex-region.test.ts` — 20 cases, all pass
- [x] `pnpm test extensions/google/vertex-provider-catalog.test.ts` — 7 cases, all pass
- [x] `pnpm test src/infra/gemini-auth.test.ts` — 5 cases, all pass
- [x] Live: `google-vertex/gemini-3-flash-preview` with `GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION=global` and `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT` set — correct Vertex URL, Bearer auth, successful response

target.title: feat: add tools.fs.roots — per-agent filesystem roots with access modes
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `tools.fs.workspaceOnly` is binary, so operators cannot safely allow agents to read or write a specific host path outside the workspace without opening broader filesystem access.
- Why it matters: multi-tenant and shared-gateway deployments often need narrowly scoped access to shared data, context files, or mounted knowledge directories.
- What changed: added `tools.fs.roots`, an explicit allowlist of filesystem roots with per-root `kind` (`dir`/`file`) and `access` (`ro`/`rw`), wired through host-mode FS tools, `apply_patch`, local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery paths, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Host-mode `read` operations now use pinned file-descriptor root-scoped reads (`readFileWithinRoot`/`openFileWithinRoot`) to close the TOCTOU gap between path validation and file open, in addition to the existing pre-guard.
- Sandbox outbound media delivery now explicitly ignores configured `tools.fs.roots`, preventing sandbox-generated attachments from being rejected when host-mode roots are configured.
- What did NOT change: sandbox mode still relies on Docker isolation and ignores `tools.fs.roots`; `exec` sandboxing is unchanged; `tools.allow` / `tools.deny` behavior is unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: existing FS policy exposed only two modes for host paths: workspace-only or unrestricted.
- Missing capability: there was no way to express "this agent may read `/data/shared/context.md` but may not read the rest of the host".
- Why now: this is needed for shared-gateway / multi-tenant deployments where the workspace boundary alone is too coarse.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: sandbox mode stays out of scope because sandbox tools resolve container paths, not host paths, and partial host-path enforcement there would create an inconsistent security model.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should catch this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests/files:
  - `src/agents/tool-fs-policy.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.multi-root-guard.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/apply-patch.test.ts`
  - `src/media/local-roots.test.ts`
  - `src/media/load-options.test.ts`
  - `src/media/local-media-access.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-safe.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-pinned-write-helper.spawn-error.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-safe.unlink-helper-error.test.ts`
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts`
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts`
  - `extensions/feishu/src/media.test.ts`
  - `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/reply-delivery.test.ts`
- Scenarios locked in:
  - lexical dir/file root matching and ro/rw enforcement
  - canonical-root precedence over alias roots for host-mode mutation paths
  - unlink/delete behavior preserving symlink-entry semantics while still respecting stricter canonical roots
  - roots precedence over `workspaceOnly` for host-mode FS access
  - sandbox-mode fallback still honoring `workspaceOnly`
  - rooted local image/PDF/reference-image loads outside the workspace
  - rooted outbound local-media and reply-delivery paths preserving file-vs-dir root semantics
  - rooted prompt-image auto-load outside the workspace
  - host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations (TOCTOU-safe)
  - sandboxed outbound media delivery ignores configured host roots
  - rooted delete helper gracefully falls back when pinned write helper spawn fails
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the risky surfaces here are policy resolution, host-path mutation, and media path propagation, so targeted seam tests catch the actual failure modes without requiring a full live deployment.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config surface: `tools.fs.roots` on global `tools.fs` and per-agent `tools.fs`.
- Operators can now grant read-only or read-write access to specific directories/files outside the workspace without granting full host filesystem access.
- Host-mode `read` / `write` / `edit` / `apply_patch` now respect those explicit roots.
- Local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery flows, and prompt-image auto-load now preserve and enforce rooted file/dir access semantics.
- When both `roots` and `workspaceOnly` are set, `roots` takes precedence for host-mode FS access and a warning is logged.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - This adds a new operator-controlled path restriction model for host-mode FS access. Risk is policy misunderstanding or incomplete enforcement across FS-adjacent surfaces. Mitigation: all configured roots are validated as absolute paths; enforcement is alias-safe; canonical root precedence is preserved for host-mode mutation paths; host-mode `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load are covered; host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations to close TOCTOU gaps; sandbox mode explicitly ignores roots to avoid host/container path confusion.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15 / darwin 25.3.0
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A for core FS policy; targeted local media tests use mocked image/PDF/image-generation providers
- Integration/channel (if any): host-mode FS tools + local media loading + outbound local-media reply flows
- Relevant config (redacted):

```json5
{
  "tools": {
    "fs": {
      "roots": [
        { "path": "/opt/app/workspaces/agent-1", "kind": "dir", "access": "rw" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/invoices", "kind": "dir", "access": "ro" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/context.md", "kind": "file", "access": "ro" }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

### Steps

1. Configure an agent with `tools.fs.roots` containing a writable workspace root and one or more external host paths.
2. Attempt `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch` and local media access both inside and outside those roots.
3. Attempt outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows with both dir roots and exact file roots.
4. Attempt prompt-image auto-load from a rooted external image path.
5. Attempt the same config in sandbox mode.

### Expected

- Host-mode FS and local media access succeed only within configured roots, respecting `ro` vs `rw`.
- Outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows preserve file-vs-dir root semantics and explicit deny-all behavior.
- Prompt-image auto-load accepts explicitly rooted host paths.
- Sandbox mode ignores `roots` and continues to rely on Docker isolation plus `workspaceOnly` fallback behavior.

### Actual

- Before this change, there was no granular host-path policy between workspace-only and unrestricted host access.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`):
  - merged `origin/main` at `631552c554`
  - `pnpm config:docs:gen`
  - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`
  - `pnpm check`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.memory-flush-forwarding.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/media/read-capability.test.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/media/read-capability.test.ts src/media/local-roots.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts -t "sends transcriptPrompt visibly and queues runtime context as hidden custom context"`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts -t "ignores host roots for sandboxed read/write/edit tools"`
- Additional focused regressions were exercised during review for:
  - fs-root resolution / precedence
  - alias-safe host mutations and unlink/delete behavior
  - media root propagation and explicit deny-all handling
  - prompt-image and local-media rooted access
  - outbound local-media / reply-delivery adapters
  - pinned-FD host reads (TOCTOU closure)
  - sandbox outbound media ignoring configured roots
- What you did **not** verify on `7b771d448d`:
  - full local `pnpm test`
  - `pnpm build`
  - live end-to-end multi-tenant deployment behavior beyond local seam tests
  - `pnpm check:changed` did not complete locally: it passed type/lint/guard phases and 2,450 tests before a local Vitest gateway worker hit `ERR_WORKER_OUT_OF_MEMORY`

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: remove `tools.fs.roots` from config or revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: `src/agents/pi-tools.ts`, `src/agents/tool-fs-policy.ts`, `src/agents/pi-tools.multi-root-guard.ts`, `src/agents/apply-patch.ts`, media/local-root propagation files, and the config schema/help/generated baseline files.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: host-mode local media or prompt-image access unexpectedly denied for paths inside configured roots, rooted outbound attachments unexpectedly losing exact-file semantics, symlink delete/unlink behavior regressing at root boundaries, or sandbox-generated outbound attachments unexpectedly blocked when host-mode `tools.fs.roots` is configured.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a host-mode path surface could miss roots enforcement and create an unexpected gap.
  - Mitigation: added targeted regression tests for host tools, `apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Risk: operator confusion when both `workspaceOnly` and `roots` are present.
  - Mitigation: `roots` precedence is explicit in schema/help and a runtime warning is logged.
- Risk: `exec` still bypasses FS roots through shell commands.
  - Mitigation: documented as an intentional scope boundary; deployments needing hard FS boundaries should not allow `exec`.

## AI assistance

- [x] AI-assisted
- Testing: locally revalidated on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`) with `pnpm check`, focused media/WhatsApp review-thread regressions, and the two prior PR-specific filtered tests; full `pnpm test` and `pnpm build` were not rerun on this final merged head; GitHub CI is green on `7b771d448d` (74 successful, 9 skipped) after the latest review fix.




## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `tools.fs.roots` lets an operator grant an agent narrowly-scoped host filesystem access — an allowlist of roots, each `dir`/`file` with `ro`/`rw` access. Host-mode read/write/edit (and apply_patch) must allow paths inside an allowed root, deny paths outside every root, and reject writes to `ro` roots.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `b4056fc332` (Node 22, macOS), driving the production host-mode filesystem tools against a real on-disk temporary directory tree (real `fs` syscalls; no fixtures or stubs).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created a real temp tree (an `rw` root `shared-rw`, an `ro` root `shared-ro`, and a `secret/` directory outside every root with a real file in it), built the production tool set via `createOpenClawCodingTools({ config: { tools: { fs: { roots: [...] } } } })`, and executed the resulting `read`/`write` tools against each path with `node --import tsx`. Live console output below.
- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
config tools.fs.roots:
  - { path: /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-rw, kind: dir, access: rw }
  - { path: /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro, kind: dir, access: ro }
workspace (not a root): /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/workspace
forbidden dir (not a root): /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret

=== host-mode reads ===
READ  inside rw root  (shared-rw/notes.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, content: "rw-root content"
READ  inside ro root  (shared-ro/policy.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, content: "ro-root content"
READ  OUTSIDE roots   (secret/passwords.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret/passwords.txt' is outside allowed filesystem roots

=== host-mode writes ===
WRITE inside rw root  (shared-rw/created.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, on disk: true
WRITE inside ro root  (shared-ro/hack.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro/hack.txt' is inside read-only root '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro'  (on disk: false)
WRITE OUTSIDE roots   (secret/exfil.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret/exfil.txt' is outside allowed filesystem roots  (on disk: false)

verify: secret/passwords.txt still says: "TOP SECRET — outside every allowed root"
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Reads inside the `rw` and `ro` roots returned the real file contents. A read of `secret/passwords.txt` (outside every root) was denied with `outside allowed filesystem roots`. A write inside the `rw` root landed on disk (`on disk: true`); a write to the `ro` root was denied (`inside read-only root`) and a write outside every root was denied — both left nothing on disk (`on disk: false`), and the out-of-root secret file was byte-for-byte unchanged afterward.
- **What was not tested:** Sandbox mode is out of scope by design — sandbox ignores `tools.fs.roots` and relies on Docker isolation — so it was not exercised here. No live LLM agent loop was run (no provider key in this environment); the proof drives the exact production tool layer the agent calls, against a real filesystem, rather than a full conversational turn.

target.title: feat(session): add chat-channel session switching with LRU history retention
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Chat channels did not support listing or switching between prior sessions under the same SessionKey, and follow-up fixes were needed to preserve switchable transcripts until LRU eviction and restore per-session usage/compaction snapshots correctly.
- Why it matters: Without in-chat session switching, users cannot navigate past conversations from Feishu/DingTalk/Telegram/Discord-style chat surfaces; incorrect transcript/archive or snapshot behavior would also make `/session back`, `/sessions`, and `/status`-adjacent state feel unreliable.
- What changed: Added `/sessions`, `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for chat channels; introduced per-SessionKey LRU `sessionHistory`; preserved switchable transcripts until history eviction; restored token/context/compaction snapshots on switch; documented matching/order/retention behavior; cleaned up reset semantics so `session.historyLimit=0` truly disables switchable history.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not change SessionKey routing, does not add cross-SessionKey switching, does not add named sessions/UI picker flows, and does not change channel plugin-specific transport behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #9959
- Related #28168

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

- Added `/sessions` to list switchable sessions for the current chat SessionKey.
- Added `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for in-chat session switching.
- Current session is included in `/sessions`, marked as `[current]`.
- `/sessions` now shows compact single-line preview rows with session ID prefix and truncated preview text.
- Session switching now restores stored token/context/compaction snapshots for the target session.
- `session.historyLimit` now consistently supports `0` as “disable switchable session history”.
- Transcripts for sessions still in history remain switchable; transcripts are archived when evicted from the LRU history queue.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw dev runtime / source tree
- Model/provider: N/A for core session-switch logic
- Integration/channel (if any): chat-channel command flow; manually validated in a real chat session
- Relevant config (redacted):
  - `session.historyLimit: 5` (default path)
  - `session.historyLimit: 0` (disable-history regression path)

### Steps

1. Start a chat conversation and create multiple sessions with `/new`.
2. Run `/sessions`, then switch via `/session <number>` or `/session back`.
3. Continue creating sessions until the history limit is exceeded.

### Expected

- `/sessions` lists current + recent switchable sessions in LRU order.
- Switching restores the target session’s stored metadata/snapshots.
- Sessions still present in history remain switchable.
- The oldest session is archived only after LRU eviction.
- With `session.historyLimit=0`, switchable history is disabled and the previous session is archived immediately on reset.

### Actual

- Behavior matches the expected results above in targeted tests and manual validation.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `/sessions` output in chat
  - `/session back` restores the previous session
  - switching to a still-retained historical session restores expected context/state
  - LRU eviction archives only the oldest evicted session
  - daily reset path preserves switchable history
  - `session.historyLimit=0` disables switchable history and archives immediately on reset
- Edge cases checked:
  - history boundary behavior at the configured limit
  - selecting current session is a no-op
  - unique-prefix SessionId selection behavior
  - stale/daily-reset session rollover
- What you did **not** verify:
  - every individual chat provider/plugin surface
  - cross-SessionKey switching (out of scope)
  - any UI/card-based session picker (out of scope)

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
  - revert this PR, or set `session.historyLimit=0` to disable switchable session history
- Files/config to restore:
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts`
  - `src/config/sessions/types.ts`
  - `src/config/types.base.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - `/sessions` shows stale/incorrect ordering
  - switched sessions lose expected token/context/compaction snapshot state
  - transcripts are archived too early while still present in history
  - `session.historyLimit=0` does not disable switchable history

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: LRU eviction/archive timing could regress and archive a session that should still remain switchable.
  - Mitigation: Added/updated targeted tests covering retained-history vs evicted-history behavior.

- Risk: Snapshot restore could leave token/context/compaction state inconsistent after switching.
  - Mitigation: Added lifecycle tests covering restore and missing-metadata fallback behavior.

- Risk: Docs could drift from implementation again as follow-up fixes land.
  - Mitigation: Updated `docs/design/chat-session-switch.md` to match the current implementation semantics in this PR.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted: Yes

I used an AI assistant for code review, doc cleanup, and targeted test updates. I manually reviewed the final diff, understood the changes, and verified the session-switch flows myself.

target.title: fix: prioritize chatAvatarUrl over assistant agent route to avoid 401
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves Fixes an issue where users who set an `Avatar` in their `IDENTITY.md` document would see a broken avatar image (401 response) in Settings → Personal → Assistant on the control-ui page. The avatar route built from the assistant agent ID (`/avatar/`) was being preferred over the `chatAvatarUrl` provided by the profile document. ## Why This Change Was Made Two issues in the `configAssistantAvatarUrl` fallback chain: 1. **Priority order**: `buildAssistantAvatarRoute` was checked before `state.chatAvatarUrl`, so the agent-based route always took precedence over the IDENTITY.md avatar. 2. **Protected route fallback**: Even after reordering, when `chatAvatarUrl` was null and the avatar status was "local", the code fell back to `buildAssistantAvatarRoute`, which emits `/avatar/` — a route protected by bearer auth. Browser `` tags cannot attach bearer headers, so this path always returns 401. The proper fix is both: prioritize `chatAvatarUrl` first, and remove the protected route fallback entirely. When the avatar is local and no `chatAvatarUrl` is set, return `null` instead of a route that requires authentication. ## User Impact Users who have configured an avatar in `IDENTITY.md` will now see it correctly rendered in Settings → Personal → Assistant instead of a broken image. Users without a profile avatar and a local assistant avatar will see no avatar (null) instead of a broken 401 image. ## Changes ### `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` - Prioritize `state.chatAvatarUrl` before the agent route check - Remove `buildAssistantAvatarRoute()` fallback — return `null` when `configAssistantAvatarStatus === "local"` instead of building a protected `/avatar/` URL ### `ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts` - Update the "falls back to assistant agent route" test to expect `null` for local status without `chatAvatarUrl` - Rename test to reflect the new behavior: "returns null for configAssistantAvatarUrl when chatAvatarUrl is null and avatar status is local (protected route)" ## Evidence Tests: - `app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`: 22 passed - `config-quick.test.ts`: 15 passed F12 Network screenshot, there is no longer a 401 error interface: Control UI Setting page screenshot, the avatar is displayed normally: Closes #85750

target.title: fix(cli): fall back to ClawHub when npm install fails for official external plugins
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

After PR #95683 externalized searxng/tavily from bundled to official npm packages, installing these plugins fails because the live npm packages (`@openclaw/searxng-plugin@0.0.0`, `@openclaw/tavily-plugin@0.0.0`) are reservation stubs without OpenClaw plugin metadata. The installer expects plugin ID `searxng` but the stub package resolves to `@openclaw/searxng-plugin`, causing an ID mismatch.

The catalog already declares both `npmSpec` and `clawhubSpec` for these plugins, but the official external install path only tries npm with no ClawHub fallback.

## Changes

- **`src/cli/plugin-install-plan.ts`**: Include `clawhubSpec` in `OfficialExternalPluginLookup` type and `resolveOfficialExternalInstallPlanBeforeNpm` return value
- **`src/cli/plugins-install-command.ts`**: Pass `clawhubSpec` through the official external plugin callback; after npm install failure for an official external plan, try ClawHub fallback when `clawhubSpec` is available; log informational message about the fallback
- **`src/cli/plugin-install-plan.test.ts`**: Added test for `clawhubSpec` passthrough in the plan resolver

## Evidence

**Behavior matrix:**

| Scenario | Before | After |
|----------|--------|-------|
| npm succeeds | ✅ Works | ✅ No change |
| npm fails, no clawhubSpec | ❌ Exit 1 | ❌ Exit 1 (no change) |
| npm fails, clawhubSpec succeeds | ❌ Exit 1 | ✅ Installs from ClawHub |
| npm fails, clawhubSpec also fails | ❌ Exit 1 (npm error only) | ❌ Exit 1 (both errors shown) |

**Code path validation:**

The fallback follows the same pattern as the existing standalone `clawhubSpec` install path (line ~1301 in `plugins-install-command.ts`), reusing `installPluginFromClawHub`, `persistPluginInstall`, and `buildClawHubPluginInstallRecordFields` — all of which are already tested in CI.

**Catalog verification:**
```json
// scripts/lib/official-external-plugin-catalog.json — searxng entry
{
  "clawhubSpec": "clawhub:@openclaw/searxng-plugin",
  "npmSpec": "@openclaw/searxng-plugin"
}
```

Both install sources are declared but only `npmSpec` was previously used in the official external plan path. This fix connects the `clawhubSpec` as a fallback.

**Lint/format:** `oxlint` 0 warnings, 0 errors. `oxfmt --check` passes.

Fixes #96878
